title: ImageWAM: Do World Action Models Really Need Video Generation, or Just Image Editing?
summary: ImageWAM asks whether World Action Models (WAMs) really need video generation to bridge visual world modeling and robot control — full future-video prediction is expensive, spends capacity on action-irrelevant appearance, and can hallucinate misleading frames. Instead, ImageWAM uses a pretrained image-editing model as the policy backbone (editing is naturally source-grounded, instruction-guided, and change-centric), but at inference it never decodes the edited frame: it takes the KV caches from a single image-editing forward pass as compact world-action context and feeds them to a flow-matching action expert that generates future robot actions. It reports strong policy performance — 93.38% on RoboTwin 2.0, 98.4% on LIBERO, and 83.1% on LIBERO-Plus — while saving 84.7% of FLOPs and giving a 4.1x latency speedup over video-generation WAMs.
